fixes.title: [knowledge] Outbound Memory sync — user-facing master→replica push to vendor native memories (per-vendor adapters, conflict policy, private-memory exclusion)
fixes.gap: Brief headline promise (rgBOQh31p3, canonical): 'outbound memory sync' — the un-lock-in layer. Workgraph FR-MEM27/16 cover INTERNAL agent-state only (self-labeled 'distinct from the user-memory product'). No story anywhere orchestrates active outbound push-sync of user-facing Orvex Memory to vendor native memories: conflict policy (OQ3, 3z78laG6dB open — Orvex master vs vendor divergence), private-memory exclusion + per-use consent, per-vendor adapter selection (Claude native memory API first per FR-D6; ChatGPT via the F1 extension until an API exists). Canon assignment: P1 prompt yXUWpQpRjx W3 routes outbound sync → knowledge (the one retrieval/sync backbone); the ai/mcp share of vendor adapters is a pack-review must-resolve, flag it.

fixes.title: [ui] AI-privacy setup — verify the connected assistant won't train on your data (connections surface, honest unsupported states)
fixes.gap: Brief (rgBOQh31p3, canonical): 'AI-privacy setup — verify the connected assistant won't train on your data' — distinct from Orvex's own no-train provider config (workgraph NFR-MEM7, which governs Orvex's OWN model providers) and from the api Private-Memory guard. Zero stories cover a user-facing check of a THIRD-PARTY connected assistant's training/retention posture. Belongs on the ui Connections surface (extends the existing 'Connections & MCP setup' story area); the backend share (per-vendor posture registry — likely ai) is a pack-review must-resolve, flag it.

fixes.milestone: B6 — Phase-2 curation surfaces (Demo World, Import, Chat History, Curator)
fixes.title: [ui] Task-first wizard — the interview surface (say the task → guided Q&A → marketplace scan → compose)
fixes.gap: Brief (rgBOQh31p3): the task-first wizard prompt builder is a headline feature. ai carries 'Wizard task-first prompt-builder AI-assist backend' but NO ui story renders the wizard interview experience (task capture → 'do you want this?' Q&A → marketplace scan hits → compose handoff into Builder/Composer). Census-brief confirmed the UI half unowned.

fixes.milestone: B2 — Skill domain, library, marketplace & social
fixes.title: [api] Orvex rating — multi-factor assembly + serving (structural AI factor × usage/popularity × upvotes) on every marketplace prompt
fixes.gap: Brief (rgBOQh31p3): the Orvex rating is multi-factor — structural quality + popularity/usage + upvotes — on every marketplace prompt. ai owns only the structural-quality AI scoring factor ('ai's share'); api owns the reputation ledger/upvotes. NO story assembles the composite rating or serves it on marketplace surfaces. api (marketplace owner) assembles; inputs cited from ai's factor + api social signals.

fixes.milestone: B2 — Skill domain, library, marketplace & social
fixes.title: [api] Marketplace seeding — GitHub import → vet → claim pipeline with the permissive-license two-gate (POC pipeline carried forward)
fixes.gap: Brief (rgBOQh31p3): marketplace seeding via the POC's GitHub-import → vet → claim pipeline with a permissive-license two-gate. api's skill/marketplace stories cover model/library/Builder/social/reputation but NO story carries the seeding pipeline or the license gates. Census-brief confirmed unowned.

fixes.title: [api] FR-O1 first-run seeding — profession-aware starter Memory + seeded demo world in the first session
fixes.gap: Fold-in audit: FR-O1 (g9vWbSYplh F0, Tier-1: 'a new user MUST reach a first useful, memory-enriched output within their first session via a guided seed producing starter Memory + a seeded demo world', per onboarding/demo-data research axvs1ZzxGn) is in NO plan.json covers[]. Adjacent-but-untagged: ui 'Streaming FormSpec onboarding' + api 'Demo World lifecycle'. This story owns the seeding logic (profession-aware starter Memory generation + demo-world seed) in api; the ui onboarding story renders it — note the interplay.

fixes.title: [ui] FR-O3 designed empty states on every memory surface (teach the next action) + demo/real separation
fixes.gap: Fold-in audit: FR-O3 (g9vWbSYplh F0: every memory surface MUST have a designed empty state that teaches the next action, plus demo/real separation so seeded content is never mistaken for the user's own or lost on clear) is in NO plan.json covers[]. ui's generic honest-states trio + Demo World banner exist but nothing specializes empty states per memory surface with teach-next-action, nor guards demo-vs-real on clear.

fixes.title: [ui] NFR-7 i18n readiness — memory surfaces i18n-capable, English-first, EU-beachhead locale phasing
fixes.gap: Fold-in audit: NFR-7 (g9vWbSYplh cross-cutting: memory surfaces support i18n; English first, EU-beachhead languages phased; OQ7 locale priority owned by GTM) — zero i18n/locale/localization hits across all 575 story titles; the ui accessibility ship-gate is WCAG-only. This story lands the i18n scaffolding (string externalization, locale plumbing, pseudo-locale test) without committing locale order (OQ7 = 'TBD — set with GTM').

fixes.milestone: B6 — Global org→cell registry & routing (the only global component)
fixes.title: [identity] ADR-0011 #4 mint side — mirror cell/cell_epoch into Clerk org/user metadata + the Keycloak mapper (config-as-code)
fixes.gap: ADR-0011 (qZFmNWGY47, Canonical, ratified 2026-07-07) decision #4: the cell claim is carried on tokens (carrier-not-owner); the registry MINTS cell/cell_epoch into Clerk org/user metadata and a Keycloak mapper. Exhaustive verify: only the PARSE side is storied (lib pkg/auth cell-claim parse); no story wires the mint/write path or the Keycloak mapper config-as-code.

fixes.milestone: B8 — Persistence, cell-contract conformance & security ops
fixes.title: [identity] ADR-0015 ruling 7a — at-rest encryption helper (OpenBao/ESO-sourced key) for the DB-backed per-tenant Clerk config
fixes.gap: ADR-0015 (PxoUAiGN29, identity-owned tenancy DB) ruling 7a mandates an at-rest encryption helper for DB-held per-tenant Clerk secrets, key sourced via OpenBao/ESO. Verified: identity E4-S6/E8 cover authz + infra secret delivery only; no lib/identity story authors the encryption helper.

fixes.milestone: B6 — Gates — divergence, drift, AGPL-import & parity
fixes.title: [contracts] ADR-0004 #2 — cell-lint-allow trailer MUST carry a non-empty reason; empty/missing fails the gate (fix the trailer matcher + test)
fixes.gap: ADR-0004 (GABwtTr4pp, live canon) decision #2: a cell-lint-allow exemption trailer without a reason MUST fail the gate. Verified against the live cell-lint.yml (orvex-studio-contracts, ENG-1541): the trailer matcher exempts any line containing the literal substring regardless of what follows — reason-required is unenforced and untested. Small fix + regression test; audit status: partial (gate exists, rule hole).

fixes.milestone: B5 — Statelessness, revocation consumer & day-1 cell contract
fixes.title: [mcp] ADR-0020 cell-discovery routing — VERIFY + harden discover-once-pin / 308 / 421 self-heal / SOLO_CELL / fail-closed errors
fixes.gap: ADR-0020 (9KcwAG5SRg, Canonical 2026-07-10) decisions 1–6: discover-once-then-pin resolver keyed by account, 308 redirect, 421 self-heal, SOLO_CELL short-circuit, CELL_UNRESOLVED/CELL_DISCOVERY_UNAVAILABLE fail-closed errors. Audit found the code shipped (src/routing/cell-resolver.ts, discovery-client.ts @ be3cd8b) but ZERO story ACs verify any of it — E5-S3 covers only the day-1 cell contract, E7-S2 only shim-overhead/lag. Audit status: present → VERIFY.

fixes.milestone: B3 — Event catalog, envelope profiles & SSE wire contract
fixes.title: [contracts] ADR-0035 §1/§6 — TS CloudEvent envelope + per-type payload codegen (json-schema-to-typescript + ajv) — the second codegen toolchain
fixes.gap: ADR-0035 (QbEBPuKcGR, Accepted) §6 verbatim: 'TS CloudEvent types (envelope + per-type payloads) are generated per §1 and land now... they do not wait on the unshipped orvex-studio-lib TS Kafka binding.' Verified: every existing codegen story/pack AC scopes TS generation to the OpenAPI/REST toolchain (openapi-typescript+openapi-fetch) only; the JSON-Schema toolchain (json-schema-to-typescript + ajv validators) is named by the ADR and owned by nobody. Consumers: api event consumption, mcp revocation consumer.

fixes.title: [contracts] ADR-0015 ruling 7b — engine-primitives.yaml workspace-existence/HEAD read op (closes the orphan-sweep contract hole)
fixes.gap: ADR-0015 (PxoUAiGN29) ruling 7b: satellites need a cheap existence/HEAD read on engine workspaces (orphan-sweep determinism). Verified: the authored engine-primitives.yaml story (contracts E2-S1) carries a closed, audited 15-op list with no existence/HEAD read. Context: ENG-1365 (Done, Contracts project) touched this scope historically — cite it, do not assume it shipped the op (the authored contract lacks it).

fixes.milestone: B1 — Foundation, tooling & shared rendering substrate
fixes.title: [ui] orvex-ds design system — ownership + shipping home (PO must-resolve: fold into orvex-studio-ui vs separate component)
fixes.gap: Component census: canon treats orvex-ds (orvex-ds.css design tokens, light+dark, exact-match fidelity) as a first-class shippable dependency of ui (and the rewrite-same-stack ruling makes tokens the Tailwind styling contract) — but orvex-ds has NO owning repo/space/project in the program. This is a DECISION issue, not a build story: pack review (ENG-2109) must resolve where orvex-ds lives (inside orvex-studio-ui vs an 18th component), who versions it, and how satellites consume it. PO decides; do not stand up a component unilaterally.
